title: afrohybanthus buxifolius
intro: Afrohybanthus buxifolius, also known as the dwarf boxwood, is a small evergreen shrub native to tropical Africa, characterized by its compact growth habit and dark green, oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of Afrohybanthus buxifolius have been used in herbal medicine for their various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In traditional medicine, Afrohybanthus buxifolius is often prepared as a decoction by boiling the leaves and roots in water, or as a tincture by steeping the plant material in a solvent, to create a medicinal remedy.
